rationale: Foundation v2.8.0 implementation roadmap step 2 explicitly lists 'CMD AUTH v1.0 (mới — chưa có trong 17 CMD list) — 45p'.
CMD AUTH is a clean-room new CMD; R66.4/5/8/9 fit naturally into its scope (auth/session lifecycle).
Avoid third pivot of CMD6 (TS Online analyst → cmd-place → would be auth = scope dilution).
Avoid stretching CMD4 charter (parse + network + qa-core, NOT auth proper).
Grace period explicitly allowed in Foundation v2.8.0 'Migration Guide': legacy session.ts continues running while R66 ramps.
deferred_to_phase15_cmd_auth: R66.4 multi-login policy (kick_old / reject_new + 5s grace + alert_threshold)
R66.5 hijack detection (IP change / device fingerprint mismatch / geo jump > 5000km / UA change)
R66.7 GM elevated auth (2FA TOTP, 15-min elevation TTL, dual-authorization)
R66.8 login flood protection (per-IP 5/60s, per-account 5 fails/5min, whitelist)
R66.9 auth_log triggered audit (event_type per R42 pattern)
tuan_2_prep_completed_by_cmd4: R66.1 session_token (opaque 256-bit + payload + verifySessionToken with expiry-first ordering)
R66.3 anti-replay (in-memory replay_cache 10k nonce ring; persistent storage deferred to svtk_runtime)
R66.6 device_fingerprint (SHA-256 5-field canonical with JSON.stringify per-field separator)

cmd_auth_handoff_artifacts.spec_ref: cmd-parse/output/auth/r66_session_token.ts file header lists in-scope vs deferred
cmd_auth_handoff_artifacts.production_ts: 246 lines, JSDoc'd, 26 vitest tests (15 normal + 11 hardening + 10 deep-hardening) all passing
input_validation_covered: expiry-first verification order (bug#15 fix — no timing oracle on expired sessions)

next_action_for_cmd_auth: Phase 15 kickoff: new CMD AUTH author imports cmd-parse/output/auth/r66_session_token.ts as foundation, adds R66.4/5/8/9 modules alongside (not replacing).
grace_period_until: CMD AUTH v1.0 ships in Phase 15 (~1 sprint per Foundation roadmap 45-minute spec scope)
legacy_path: src/server/auth/* (login.ts / password_hash.ts / oauth_zalo.ts / session.ts) ported to cmd-parse/output/auth/ as-is — continues serving until CMD AUTH cuts over.
